parp_axis_row.mechanism: Pan-cancer screens and expression–sensitivity correlates link PARP pathway activity or PARP1 levels to drug response in HRD/DDR contexts; those signals are not MBD4-specific. Separately, some preclinical work discusses BER load or RNF144A–PARP1 routing as a hypothetical sensitization narrative in MBD4-deficient models — a distinct layer, not interchangeable with pan-tumor PARP1 biomarker claims.

falsification_test_from_atr_wee1_row.summary: Falsification arm — PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF lines. Tests the competing hypothesis that MBD4-LOF sensitivity is driven by PARP1 upregulation (PARP-trapping pathway). Result: PARP1 is NOT elevated in MBD4-LOF — Mann-Whitney U p=0.605, median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656. n_LOF=19, n_comparator=1498. Encoded POSITIVE because this *rules out* PARP-trapping as the mechanism, which IS positive evidence for ATR over PARP (justifies recommending ATR inhibitors rather than olaparib/talazoparib). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi correlation ρ=-0.416, p=1.4e-21 (n=481) confirms the framework — high PARP1 → more PARPi-sensitive is real, MBD4-LOF lines just don't show it.

narrative: PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) are NOT recommended for MBD4-LOF tumors on a transcriptional-upregulation basis. Competing hypothesis — MBD4-LOF benefits from PARPi via PARP1 transcriptional upregulation — falsified: PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF NOT elevated vs comparator (n=19 vs 1498, p=0.605). Recommended axis is ATR/WEE1 inhibition (lead: ceralasertib).
